thesis: Batch 1 expands the research to a broader ETF macro universe, a longer 2022-01-01 to 2026-03-06 sample, and a new breakout family so the campaign can rank family quality before deeper exploitation.

benchmark.purpose: Primary passive benchmark for all campaign batches

universe.name: core_us_macro_v2
universe.selection_rule: Fixed liquid US beta ETFs, defensive ETFs, approved leveraged ETF exposure, and compliance-safe references

carry_forward_findings: QQQ 40/180 + 252-day time stop is the best observed trend score so far.
Dual momentum on QQQ/VOO/TLT is the first non-trend family worth continuing.
Trailing stops were inactive on SPY but may still matter on more volatile symbols such as TQQQ.
Low-trade winners still require more structural evidence, not just better in-sample scores.

candidates.hypothesis: VOO remains the passive benchmark for the full campaign.
QQQ buy-and-hold measures passive growth beta over the same window.
TQQQ buy-and-hold measures the raw leveraged upside and drawdown envelope.
GLD buy-and-hold measures defensive commodity exposure over the window.
TLT buy-and-hold measures long-duration defense over the same sample.
SPY 40/180 with 252-day time stop remains the conservative trend control.
QQQ 40/180 with 252-day time stop remains the aggressive trend control.
QQQ/VOO/TLT dual momentum stays the cross-family rotation control.
